response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with genuine geospatial interest (founder of an open-source Geo-Data platform, web GIS work) and some systems-level experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/sec, C/C++ work on LabWindows/CVI). However, their recent career is heavily web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), their C++ experience is dated and not modern C++, and they show no direct experience with routing engines, map-tile pipelines, or navigation guidance systems. They could grow into the role given demonstrated breadth, but they are not a strong direct match for a C++/Rust routing data pipeline position.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure: founded an open-source Geo-Data/mapping platform used by Green Map and built web GIS applications
Systems-level performance experience: low-latency DLang microservices with async I/O, fibers, and caching at millions of requests/sec
Broad technical range with strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, DevOps, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes)
concerns: C++ experience is old and pre-modern-C++; no Rust experience listed, missing the primary language requirement
No experience with routing algorithms, tile generation, or navigation guidance systems specifically
Recent roles are web/full-stack product work, distant from large-scale data pipeline engineering
